title: Donnie Fatso

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer encounters some unspecified frustration or mishap
jokes.punchline: His iconic catchphrase expressing frustration
jokes.explanation: Homer's signature catchphrase - perfect character integration and economy, but completely predictable. Pure running gag with no originality.

jokes.text: I don't even want to smell sparkling apple juice again. Aw. Poor baby.
jokes.setup: Someone is hungover from New Year's Eve festivities
jokes.punchline: The irony that they're sick from non-alcoholic apple juice
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about being sick from innocent drinks, but lacks punch and character specificity.

jokes.text: Come on, Bart. Afraid of a little apple juice? Pig in a blanket! Dad's underwear. Aunt Selma's leg.
jokes.setup: Homer challenges Bart about being afraid of apple juice
jokes.punchline: Bart's random, escalating list of disgusting things he associates with hangovers
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation with specific gross-out imagery that feels authentically Bart-like.

jokes.text: I hate New Year's Eve. I can't believe I made out with Ryan Seacrest on the TV screen.
jokes.setup: Lisa expressing regret about New Year's Eve
jokes.punchline: The absurd image of prim Lisa drunkenly kissing the TV during Dick Clark's show
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - the idea of intellectual Lisa doing something this embarrassing is perfectly crafted and unexpected.

jokes.text: I didn't see you drink last night. I didn't. I got secondhand wasted from smooching your father.
jokes.setup: Lisa questioning why Marge is hungover when she didn't drink
jokes.punchline: Marge got drunk through contact with Homer's alcohol-soaked saliva
jokes.explanation: Clever concept of 'secondhand wasted' - original take on secondhand smoke with good economy and structure.

jokes.text: So not a peep out of anyone till the Fiesta Bowl. That's not for five hours. I know when the freaking Fiesta Bowl is!
jokes.setup: Marge demands quiet until the Fiesta Bowl, Homer notes it's hours away
jokes.punchline: Marge's irritated overreaction to Homer's helpful observation
jokes.explanation: Standard hungover irritability humor - works but predictable escalation pattern.

jokes.text: New Year's Eve might be over in America, but we're still rocking hard in Samoa. And here come the Blue Angels waking up the shrieking wah-wah birds.
jokes.setup: TV coverage of New Year's celebrations continuing in Samoa
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous image of military jets disturbing made-up 'shrieking wah-wah birds'
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist imagery with 'shrieking wah-wah birds' - original but lacks character connection.

jokes.text: Simpson, you've committed Springfield's first crime of the new year. Failed to separate your bottles and newspapers.
jokes.setup: Chief Wiggum dramatically announces Homer's committed the first crime of the year
jokes.punchline: It's just improper recycling - completely mundane
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - sets up serious crime then deflates with recycling violation. Classic Wiggum-style importance mismatch.

jokes.text: 50 bucks?! That used to cover two public nudities and a poop in the park.
jokes.setup: Homer shocked by the $50 fine for recycling violation
jokes.punchline: His casual reference to much more serious crimes as price comparison
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation revealing Homer's casual relationship with petty crime. Perfect economy and character voice.

jokes.text: That's vandalizing city property. What the hell?! Mild obscenity before 10:00 a.m. Also illegal.
jokes.setup: Homer gets ticketed for throwing the first ticket on ground
jokes.punchline: Even his mild swear word reaction is somehow illegal with specific time restrictions
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating absurdity with the specific time restriction detail adding extra layer of bureaucratic ridiculousness.

jokes.text: The government runs out of money, so they pass a bunch of lame-o laws to sock it to the little guy. Fine. You're telling it like it is. Also a crime now.
jokes.setup: Homer accurately diagnosing the revenue scheme
jokes.punchline: Even truth-telling about government schemes is somehow illegal
jokes.explanation: Sharp meta-commentary on authoritarianism - the government criminalizing criticism of itself. Well-structured escalation.

jokes.text: That's almost $1,000! Rounding up your total-- that's a $50 fine.
jokes.setup: Homer shocked at his accumulated fines reaching $1000
jokes.punchline: Even expressing shock at the total generates another fine
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the escalating bureaucracy gag with efficient callback structure.

jokes.text: This year's only 11 hours old, and it's already your annus horribilis. My New Year's resolution was to learn Latin.
jokes.setup: Lisa commenting on Homer's terrible day using Latin phrase
jokes.punchline: The reveal that she's showing off her New Year's resolution to learn Latin
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - showing off intellectual achievement while also being genuinely helpful. Great integration.

jokes.text: Glinda, Madame Morrible, flying monkeys there, can you tell me how I can get back to my bar? Moe, you've always had the power to get back to the bar.
jokes.setup: Moe and Homer have stumbled into what appears to be a production of Wicked
jokes.punchline: The performers stay in character and respond to Moe's question as if it's part of the show
jokes.explanation: Clever intersection of Wizard of Oz references with Moe's situation. Good visual comedy concept.

jokes.text: Whoa! It's a chimpan-me.
jokes.setup: Homer presumably seeing his reflection or a monkey
jokes.punchline: His made-up word combining 'chimpanzee' and 'me'
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Homer wordplay - dumb but endearing. Perfect character voice but low-hanging fruit.

jokes.text: You slip the guy 100 bucks, and your record is as clean as... Uh, I ain't got nothing, uh, clean to compare it to.
jokes.setup: Moe explaining corruption while trying to find a metaphor about cleanliness
jokes.punchline: His admission that nothing in his world is actually clean
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment revealing Moe's grimy world through failed metaphor. Self-aware and well-structured.

jokes.text: Ten minutes? I can't wait that long. I left an ice cream cone in my cup holder. Hold on. I will get help. Must break glass.
jokes.setup: Homer can't wait 10 minutes for service
jokes.punchline: His trivial reason (ice cream cone) and dramatic overreaction (breaking emergency glass)
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic - trivial concern leading to drastic action. Character-appropriate escalation.

jokes.text: Chief, this is just a big misunderstanding. I meant to write 'bride.' It's a wedding present. Oh, how thoughtful. Aah! My bribe!
jokes.setup: Homer tries to claim his bribe money was meant to say 'bride'
jokes.punchline: He immediately undermines his own excuse by calling it 'my bribe'
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer self-sabotage - creates plausible excuse then immediately destroys it. Well-structured escalation.

jokes.text: It's okay. It's okay. I can be out in 18 months with good behavior. We're Simpsons, Dad. We don't do good behavior.
jokes.setup: Homer optimistically talking about early release for good behavior
jokes.punchline: Bart's matter-of-fact observation about their family's nature
jokes.explanation: Brilliant family dynamics joke. Bart's deadpan honesty about their collective character flaws is perfectly crafted and quotable.

jokes.text: All that time I'll be stuck in my cell, unable to exercise. Actually, most convicts work out like crazy. I repeat: unable to exercise.
jokes.setup: Homer worried about not being able to exercise in prison
jokes.punchline: When told prisoners actually exercise more, he doubles down on his commitment to not exercising
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - presented with facts that contradict his excuse, he simply restates it more emphatically. Perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: I can't go undercover with Fat Tony. He's met me. I've been in his house. I was briefly his 'conserigliary...' consugly... canoliar... Robert Duvall.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why he can't go undercover with Fat Tony
jokes.punchline: His inability to pronounce 'consigliere' and settling for 'Robert Duvall'
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer struggling with vocabulary and finding creative workarounds. Good character voice and cultural reference.

jokes.text: Can I be Johnny Undercover? That's a little obvious. Okay. Melvin Mafia.
jokes.setup: Homer suggesting undercover names
jokes.punchline: Each suggestion is more obviously criminal than the last
jokes.explanation: Good escalating stupidity - Homer's complete misunderstanding of subtlety in undercover work.

jokes.text: You will be known as Nicky 'Blue Pants' Altosaxophony. Can I keep the name after I'm done? No. It belongs to the government.
jokes.setup: FBI gives Homer an elaborate fake mobster name
jokes.punchline: Homer wants to keep it permanently and the bureaucratic response about government ownership
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist bureaucracy humor - the idea that fake mob names are government property is wonderfully ridiculous.

jokes.text: To zoom in, you go, 'Ayyy!' To zoom out, go, 'Ohhh!' Ayyy. Ohhh! Ayyy. Ohhh! Fuhgetaboutit! That activates the auto-destruct.
jokes.setup: FBI explaining spy camera controls using stereotypical Italian-American sounds
jokes.punchline: The most stereotypical phrase activates the self-destruct sequence
jokes.explanation: Clever spy gadget parody with good escalation to the absurd self-destruct trigger. Well-structured escalation.

jokes.text: Dear Lord, watch over my sweet Homie while he's in the hoosegow. Keep him safe from shanks and shivs. Let him become rich in the jail yard currencies of cigarettes and dried fish.
jokes.setup: Marge praying for Homer's safety in prison
jokes.punchline: Her unexpectedly detailed knowledge of prison terminology and economics
jokes.explanation: Excellent character subversion - sweet Marge casually referencing prison violence and economics. Good contrast.

jokes.text: You do know the meat is made from diseased pigeons. Just get the ladle down there deep. That's where the beaks are.
jokes.setup: Prisoner warning Homer about the disgusting prison food
jokes.punchline: Homer's enthusiastic request to get to the most disgusting parts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer characterization - turning disgusting food warning into enthusiasm. Good gross-out escalation.

jokes.text: Blue pants power!
jokes.setup: Homer standing up to the fake aggressor as planned
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous superhero-style battle cry referencing his nickname
jokes.explanation: Moderately funny superhero parody but relies on reference callback rather than original humor.

jokes.text: Don't worry. We're not going to touch you. We'll make it sound like a beating by tenderizing these steaks. Can I have any of the steaks? No.
jokes.setup: FBI explaining how they'll fake the beating sounds
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately focused on whether he can eat the steaks
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer priority mismatch - focusing on food during serious explanation. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: That's so mean! Please! Let me have it! Come on! Give it to me right in the mouth!
jokes.setup: Homer begging for the steaks from the fake beating
jokes.punchline: His desperate language sounds sexual and inappropriate out of context
jokes.explanation: Moderately effective innuendo through innocent food desire, but relies on cheap double entendre.

jokes.text: He's in. Aw, for-- I was going to say that!
jokes.setup: One FBI agent announces success
jokes.punchline: Another agent's petty disappointment at not getting to say the cool line
jokes.explanation: Mild workplace humor about wanting to say the dramatic line. Functional but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: If, if Jews control the media, why can't I get on Jimmy Kimmel, huh? Huh?
jokes.setup: Krusty in drunk tank ranting about anti-Semitic conspiracy theories
jokes.punchline: His personal failure to get on a talk show disproves the conspiracy he's referencing
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical take on anti-Semitic conspiracy theories through personal failure. Good character voice and social commentary.

jokes.text: I detect a distinct lack of oregano. You carry oregano with you? Oregano, basil, rosemary... I-I think I got a ketchup packet for you, boss.
jokes.setup: Homer critiquing Fat Tony's sauce and revealing he carries spices
jokes.punchline: Louie's pathetic offering of a ketchup packet in comparison
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Homer's unexpected culinary sophistication and Louie's pathetic attempt to impress.

jokes.text: I-I-I bring my own candy to the movie theater.
jokes.setup: Fat Tony demanding to know what secrets Louie is hiding
jokes.punchline: Louie's confession to the most mundane possible 'crime'
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of tension through pathetic confession. Good contrast with mob intimidation context.

jokes.text: My beloved Anna Maria. I hope Heaven's powder room is painted that eggshell blue you could never achieve on Earth.
jokes.setup: Fat Tony visiting his wife's grave with emotional tribute
jokes.punchline: His oddly specific focus on interior decorating in the afterlife
jokes.explanation: Beautiful blend of genuine emotion with absurdly specific domestic concerns. Great character depth through humor.

jokes.text: You think an informant could get that close to me? Oh, yeah. I got in your gang, and you barely know who I am.
jokes.setup: Fat Tony skeptical about informants getting close
jokes.punchline: Homer casually proving how easy it is by describing exactly what he did
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dramatic irony - Homer accidentally confessing while trying to be helpful. Perfect economy and structure.

jokes.text: When I call for my Russian business partner, Yuri Nator, I don't expect to be screamed at like I was some ten-year-old punk making a prank call. Yuri Nator? Yuri Nator! Hey, my mouth is begging for a Yuri Nator!
jokes.setup: Fat Tony explaining anger at Russian partner named Yuri Nator
jokes.punchline: Homer realizing it sounds like 'urinator' and making bathroom humor
jokes.explanation: Straightforward pun humor. Homer-appropriate but relies on obvious wordplay without much craft.

jokes.text: Why, you... I'm going to chop you into little pieces, and make you into a Rubik's cube, which I will never solve!
jokes.setup: Yuri threatening Homer with violence
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific and ultimately futile threat involving a puzzle toy
jokes.explanation: Creative violent threat that becomes absurd through the Rubik's cube metaphor and admission of failure.

jokes.text: I was, uh, just thinking how cheap this crime would've been in the 70's, when gas was 35 cents a gallon.
jokes.setup: Homer hesitating about committing arson
jokes.punchline: His concern is purely about the economics of gasoline prices
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer logic - reducing serious crime to cost-benefit analysis. Great character voice and unexpected perspective.

jokes.text: This is horrible! I keep smelling my own breath!
jokes.setup: Homer presumably wearing some kind of mask or hood
jokes.punchline: His focus on minor discomfort rather than the serious crime happening
jokes.explanation: Good character priority mismatch - Homer fixated on trivial discomfort during serious moment. Authentic voice.

jokes.text: Nicolas Blue Pants, you have shown great fazagabool in the face of overwhelming spoogatzagatini.
jokes.setup: Fat Tony giving Homer a ceremonial speech
jokes.punchline: His use of completely made-up pseudo-Italian words that sound authentic
jokes.explanation: Clever fake-Italian gibberish that sounds plausible. Good cultural parody but relies somewhat on ethnic humor.

jokes.text: From now on, you don't know where you end and we begin. Your problems are our problems. My neck is kind of itchy. Then all of our necks are itchy.
jokes.setup: Fat Tony explaining the family bond philosophy
jokes.punchline: Homer testing it with a trivial problem and the gang's immediate solidarity
jokes.explanation: Good literal interpretation of metaphorical bonding speech. Well-structured test of the concept with absurd results.

jokes.text: To heterosexual male friendship, the kind the Greeks wrote about. Huh?
jokes.setup: Fat Tony toasting to male friendship with classical reference
jokes.punchline: Homer's confusion at the historically inaccurate description
jokes.explanation: Smart historical irony about Greek male relationships. Good intellectual humor with Homer's authentic confusion.

jokes.text: You're as nervous as a cat when Johnny Kick-A-Cat walks into the room.
jokes.setup: Fat Tony describing Homer's nervousness with a simile
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous specificity of 'Johnny Kick-A-Cat' as a mobster nickname
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist mob nickname that's both ridiculous and perfectly logical within the simile context.

jokes.text: In the strip club of my heart, you held the key to the champagne room.
jokes.setup: Fat Tony expressing betrayal and hurt to Homer
jokes.punchline: His romantic metaphor using strip club terminology
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor mixing genuine emotion with seedy venue terminology. Perfect Fat Tony character voice - touching and crude simultaneously.

jokes.text: Homer, I just want to say that of all the rat bastards we've had working for us, you were the snitchiest.
jokes.setup: FBI agent giving Homer what seems like praise
jokes.punchline: The backhanded compliment about being the best at betrayal
jokes.explanation: Standard backhanded compliment structure. Works but lacks originality or strong character voice.

jokes.text: He was just a soldier in a war he started.
jokes.setup: Homer reflecting philosophically on Fat Tony's death
jokes.punchline: The logical contradiction of being a soldier in your own war
jokes.explanation: Sharp logical observation delivered with faux-profundity. Good economy and ironic structure.

jokes.text: When I shut my eyes, all I see is Fat Tony and me having drinks at that bar on the beach. You never took me to that bar on the beach. Oh. Well, uh... you wouldn't like it; it's not very good.
jokes.setup: Homer describing traumatic visions, Marge noting inconsistency
jokes.punchline: Homer's weak attempt to cover his fabricated memory
jokes.explanation: Great married couple dynamics - Marge's practical fact-checking deflating Homer's dramatic moment. Good escalation.

jokes.text: No. I'm his cousin from San Diego-- Fit Tony. Wow, I've never seen a mobster use a track suit for exercising.
jokes.setup: New character introducing himself as Fat Tony's cousin
jokes.punchline: Homer's observation about the unexpected athletic use of typical mob attire
jokes.explanation: Good observation about mob fashion clichés being used practically. Sets up character contrast well.

jokes.text: Your tears seem in no way crocodilian.
jokes.setup: Fit Tony assessing Homer's emotional sincerity
jokes.punchline: His pretentious vocabulary choice of 'crocodilian' instead of 'crocodile tears'
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment - Fit Tony's more educated/pretentious speech pattern. Nice vocabulary humor.

jokes.text: Well. This is awkward. What? Oh, uh, Mr. Burns! Uh, you were cool, too.
jokes.setup: Homer praising Fat Tony as best boss ever
jokes.punchline: Burns appearing and Homer's panicked save attempt
jokes.explanation: Good awkward situation with characteristic Homer panic response

jokes.text: And to extinguish you would be to whack what still remains of him.
jokes.setup: Fit Tony explaining why he won't kill Homer
jokes.punchline: His elevated vocabulary mixing philosophical language with mob slang
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Fit Tony's pretentious speech mixed with crime terminology. Nice philosophical mob logic.

jokes.text: That's why I keep my friends close. And your enemies closer? No, why would I do that? If they were close, they would kill me.
jokes.setup: Homer anticipating the famous Godfather quote
jokes.punchline: Fit Tony's practical rejection of the famous strategy as illogical
jokes.explanation: Excellent deconstruction of famous movie logic through practical reasoning. Smart pop culture subversion with clear logic.

jokes.text: The pressures got to him, so he started to eat. Soon, he was known as 'Fit Fat Tony,' then just 'Fat Tony' for short.
jokes.setup: Explaining how Fit Tony transformed into Fat Tony
jokes.punchline: The gradual name evolution tracking his weight gain
jokes.explanation: Clever cyclical storytelling device with good escalation structure. Nice meta-commentary on character continuity.

jokes.text: Now I'm just an average schnook like everyone else, stuck in this backwater burg where all you can count on is your family, and the only one who ever shot anyone is the baby.
jokes.setup: Homer's philosophical conclusion about his ordinary life
jokes.punchline: The casual reference to Maggie's shooting of Mr. Burns as family violence
jokes.explanation: Great series callback with excellent deadpan delivery. Perfect blend of family sentiment and dark humor history.

tmdb_overview: Homer ends up in the Springfield penitentiary after getting caught bribing an official; an FBI investigator offers Homer a way to reduce his jail time.
